rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL J. MOATS ET AL VS. MICHAEL TSANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Joint Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 17, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT NRT WEST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION is continued to October 30, 2023 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN E. WAI VS. FATHI SAID ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE ON ONELABS TENANTS' TRO APPLICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 17, 2023 line 2. HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE ON ONELABS TENANTS' TRO APPLICATION is DENIED in part; GRANTED in part; and continued in part. The request to enjoin the landlord from collecting lease payments directly from the Mudryi Group's subtenants is continued to November 2, 2023 to be heard with the Landlord's pending motion. The TRO on this issue remains in effect until further ruling of this Court. The request to enjoin the landlord from signing new lease agreement or placing new tenants in Mudryi's Group vacant units is denied as it does not appear to be an outstanding issue in the supplemental briefing filed in response to Court's October 10, 2023 order. The request to enjoin the landlord from changing the locks and entry codes without 24-hour advanced notice is GRANTED. Any changes to the locks/codes must be on a 24-hour notice. Request to provide current keys and access codes is DENIED. The issue is moot per the supplemental briefing submitted by the moving party. Request to return all internet equipment and cancel internet service accounts is DENIED. The issue is moot per the supplemental briefing submitted by the moving party. The issues regarding master code, return of locks and maintenance requests are not properly before the Court at this time per the Notice of TRO and OSC issued thereon. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRICK & TIMBER, INC. VS. TODD JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 17, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT TODD JOHNSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to serve a 37.9(o) Notice in advance of service of the operative 10 Day Notice to Quit. 10 Day Warning Notice serviced on November 16, 2022 in advance of the previously served 10 Day Notice to Cure or Quit, which formed the basis of the previously dismissed unlawful detainer action, is not sufficient to support a subsequently filed unlawful detainer action. Given the particular procedural posture of this case the landlord was required to serve a 10 Day Notice before serving a new notice of termination of tenancy. Since the November 16, 2022 Warning notice was served, the landlord (1) served a Notice to Cure or Quit alleging nuisance from "October 2022 to Date [November 29, 2022], (2) filed an unlawful detainer on December 15, 2022; (3) allegedly accepted rent during the pendency of that unlawful detainer for January and February 2023; and (4) dismissed the December 15, 2022 unlawful detainer action on February 17, 2023 without prejudice. Plaintiff did not re-file the unlawful detainer based on the previously served Notice to Cure or Quit. Instead, on March 22, 2023 the landlord served the operative Notice to Quit covering an additional period of time from "October 2022 to Date [March 22, 2023]." Therefore, based on all the intervening factors between the service of the 10 Day Warning and the issuance of the operative Notice to Quit, the previously served 10 Day Warning is not in compliance with the requirements of SFRO 37.9(o). The Court agrees with the landlord that the fundamental task in construing a statute is to ascertain the intent of the lawmakers, so as to effectuate the purpose of the statute and that the 10 Day Warning Notice must be served to give the tenant an opportunity to cure before the Plaintiff proceeds with recovery of possession. The November 16, 2022 Warning cannot be "re-cycled" in this action covering an additional period of time the same as Plaintiff chosing to not "re-cycle" the original 10 Day Notice to Cure or Quit. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
